stories.headline: Iran war live: Tehran’s FM in Islamabad; US says envoys to travel for talks

stories.headline: West Bank and part of Gaza vote in first municipal elections since war
stories.summary: Municipal elections in the West Bank and central Gaza mark the first vote since the Gaza war, with Fatah-linked lists dominant and Hamas absent from the race.

stories.headline: Polls open in Gaza area in first municipal election in 20 years
stories.summary: Gaza officials say Deir el-Balah vote is a 'pilot', while occupied West Bank also goes to first polls since 2023 war.

stories.headline: Palestinians cast their ballots as Gaza holds first election in 21 years
stories.summary: Palestinians are voting in Gaza’s first municipal elections in 21 years. Around 70,000 people are registered to vote.

stories.headline: West Bank scepticism as Palestinians doubt local elections will change much
stories.summary: Frustration with the Palestinian Authority and Israeli occupation fuel voter apathy in local West Bank elections.

stories.headline: Israel escalates attacks in Gaza, killing 12 people
stories.summary: Israeli forces have killed 12 Palestinians, including six police officers, in multiple attacks across Gaza.

stories.headline: 31 sloths die in Florida before opening of attraction
stories.summary: Many died due to conditions at a Florida warehouse where they'd been shipped, while others arrived from South America dead, authorities say.

stories.headline: Iran says no direct talks planned as U.S. envoys set to leave for Pakistan
stories.summary: Iran’s foreign minister arrived in Islamabad for talks with Pakistani officials. The White House said Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner would leave for Islamabad on Saturday.

stories.headline: Iran’s FM Abbas Araghchi meets with Pakistan officials but rules out direct talks with US
stories.summary: US special envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are expected to arrive in Pakistan on Saturday ahead of a second round of peace talks between the US and Iran. Tehran, however, has ruled out direct talks.

stories.headline: Inside Chornobyl: 40 years after disaster, nuclear site still at risk in Russia’s war
stories.summary: In February 2025, a cheap Russian drone tore through Chornobyl’s confinement shelter. Workers warn the site of the world’s worst nuclear accident is not safe yetThe dosimeter clipped to your chest ticks faster the moment you step off the designated path inside the Chornobyl nuclear power plant. stories.headline: China Says US Export Bills Risk Disrupting Chip Supply Chains
stories.summary: China said it is closely monitoring US legislative plans after the House Foreign Affairs Committee advanced export-control bills targeting semiconductors, which Beijing warned could disrupt global supply chains if passed.

stories.headline: Xi Calls Laos Key Priority in China’s Neighborhood Diplomacy
stories.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping said that Beijing has always viewed its relations with Laos from a strategic and long-term perspective, describing the country as a key priority in China’s diplomacy with neighboring nations.

stories.headline: Tax the Rich Policies Draw Attention
stories.summary: Michael Bologna, Bloomberg Industry Group senior tax correspondent, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Real Yield." Democrats around the country are pushing forward on plans to tax the rich — moves that are supportive for the tax-exempt municipal-bond market.   (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.summary: Belgium was cut by S&P Global Ratings, the second downgrade by a credit assessor in a week on the country running the euro area’s biggest budget deficits.

stories.summary: Gov. Janet Mills said she rejected what would have been the nation’s first moratorium on data centers because it failed to exempt a project in a distressed mill town.
